hero.title: Greatness in Intimacy
hero.subtitle: Dutch-language education in the heart of Nairobi. Where every child is seen, known, and valued.

about.title: About the Dutch School Nairobi
about.subtitle: More than 50 years of educational excellence
about.description: The Dutch School has been offering Dutch-language education in Nairobi for more than 50 years. Our small classrooms focus on individual learning needs, while our large, green compound provides a wonderful place to learn and discover.
about.missionText: We provide international, multilingual, and differentiated education to toddlers and primary school students that fully meets the Dutch Core Objectives and the goals of the International Primary Curriculum, enabling our students to develop into open-minded, inquiring, and critical global citizens.
about.visionText: Our vision is to develop children's own identity and talents to make a valuable contribution to a changing world.

features.title: Why Choose the Dutch School?
features.subtitle: Greatness in intimacy - where every child is seen

bilingual.title: Bilingual Education
bilingual.description: Fully bilingual curriculum for seamless transition, internationally or in the Netherlands and Belgium.

curriculum.title: IEYC/IPC Curriculum
curriculum.description: International Early Years and Primary Curriculum with thematic learning that encourages inquiry-based learning and global citizenship.

community.title: Small Community
community.description: With 10+ nationalities and small class sizes, every child receives personalized attention and care.

environment.title: Green Campus
environment.description: A beautiful green compound in Karen with space for outdoor learning, play, experience, and discovery.

programs.title: Our Offering
programs.subtitle: A varied offering that meets diverse needs

toddler.title: Toddler Group
toddler.description: Learning through play and discovery in a warm and safe environment, in small groups (max. 15 toddlers), with a strong language foundation in Dutch and English.

primary.title: Primary School
primary.description: Rich bilingual education combining Dutch curriculum with International Primary Curriculum (IPC).

ntc.title: NTC
ntc.description: Dutch language and culture education in the afternoon for children attending other international schools in Nairobi.

adult.title: Adult Dutch Lessons
adult.description: Dutch language courses for adults at all proficiency levels. Perfect for parents, partners, or anyone interested in learning Dutch.

testimonial1.quote: The Dutch School has been a second home for our children. The bilingual approach has given them confidence in both Dutch and English, and the small class sizes mean they truly thrive.
testimonial1.author: Maria van der Berg

testimonial2.quote: Moving from the Netherlands, we were worried about our children's education. The Dutch School exceeded all expectations - they've maintained their Dutch while embracing their international experience.
testimonial2.author: Peter & Anna Jansen

testimonial3.quote: The IPC curriculum combined with Dutch traditions creates a unique learning environment. Our daughter loves school and is developing into a true global citizen.
testimonial3.author: Sarah Okonkwo

quickFacts.title: Quick Facts About the Dutch School

q1.question: The Dutch School in a nutshell
q1.answer: The Dutch School is an accredited school in Nairobi, Kenya, with various departments. The bilingual primary school offers Dutch-language and English-language education for children from age 1.5. Established in 1971 and recognized by the Dutch Education Abroad Foundation (NOB), the school combines the Dutch national curriculum with the International Primary Curriculum (IPC).

q2.question: What curriculum does the Dutch School follow?
q2.answer: We follow a bilingual curriculum combining the official Dutch core objectives with the IEYC (International Early Years Curriculum) and IPC (International Primary Curriculum). This ensures students can transition seamlessly to schools in the Netherlands and Belgium or continue their international education pathway in Kenya or elsewhere.

q3.question: What are the school fees at the Dutch School?
q3.answer: Our fees are divided into two groups: Tariff A for families where the employer reimburses school fees, and Tariff B for families paying out of pocket. Contact us for the detailed fee structure.

q4.question: Do students need to speak Dutch to enroll?
q4.answer: No prior Dutch language knowledge is required for our day school department. Our immersive bilingual program supports children at all language levels. In most cases, students speak fluent Dutch in daily use within a year, through the combination of daily exposure and dedicated language instruction.

hero.title: About Dutch School Nairobi
hero.subtitle: Greatness in intimacy - nearly 50 years of educational excellence in Kenya

history.title: Our History
history.content: Founded in 1971, the Netherlands School Society has been a cornerstone of Dutch education in East Africa. What started as a small initiative by Dutch expatriate families has grown into a thriving international community, proudly recognized by the Dutch Education Abroad Foundation.

timeline.1971: School founded by Dutch expatriate families

approach.title: Our Educational Approach
approach.content: We believe in the power of small-scale education. Our unique combination of the Dutch curriculum and the International Primary Curriculum (IPC) ensures students are well-prepared for both Dutch and international educational pathways. Small class sizes allow for personalized attention, ensuring every child reaches their full potential.

team.title: Our Team
team.subtitle: Dedicated educators passionate about your child's success

value1.title: Connect
value1.description: We create strong bonds between students, parents, and teachers in a close-knit community.

value2.title: Inspire
value2.description: We spark curiosity and a lifelong love of learning.

value3.title: Move Together
value3.description: We adapt to the unique needs of each child and situation.

value4.title: Global Citizenship
value4.description: We prepare children to thrive in an interconnected world.

cta.title: Ready to Join Our Community?

hero.title: Our Vision & Mission
hero.subtitle: Greatness in Intimacy - where every child is seen, known, and valued

philosophy.title: Small Makes Great
philosophy.content: At Dutch School Nairobi, we believe that small makes great. In an intimate setting where every child is personally known, we can provide the best environment for development and growth.
philosophy.quote: Greatness in Intimacy means we are great in what we do, precisely because we are small in size.

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.content: To provide high-quality bilingual education in a small-scale, warm environment where every child can develop optimally and be prepared for an international future.

vision.title: Our Vision
vision.content: A school where every child is seen, known, and valued. Where the Dutch language and culture form a solid foundation and children develop into confident global citizens.

connect.title: Connect
connect.description: We build strong relationships between school, home, and community. Connection is the foundation of everything we do.

inspire.title: Inspire
inspire.description: We stimulate curiosity, creativity, and a lifelong love of learning in every child.

move.title: Move Together
move.description: We are flexible and adapt to the unique needs of each child and situation.

cta.title: Experience Our Vision in Practice
cta.subtitle: Come for a tour and discover what small-scale education can mean for your child.

hero.title: Our Team
hero.subtitle: Dedicated professionals who know every child personally

intro.title: The People Behind Our School
intro.content: Our small, close-knit team consists of experienced and passionate professionals. Due to our small scale, every staff member knows every child personally, ensuring optimal guidance and support.

leadership.title: School Leadership
leadership.description: Our school leader carries daily responsibility for education and the wellbeing of all students.

teachers.title: Teachers
teachers.description: Our qualified teachers combine Dutch educational experience with international expertise. They create a safe, stimulating learning environment.

support.title: Support Staff
support.description: From learning support coordinator to administration - our team works together to ensure the best experience for children and parents.

cta.title: Meet Our Team
cta.subtitle: Schedule a visit and meet the people who will guide your child.

hero.title: Location & Campus
hero.subtitle: A green oasis in the heart of Karen, Nairobi

location.title: Our Location
location.content: Dutch School Nairobi is located in Karen, one of the greenest and most peaceful neighborhoods of Nairobi. Our spacious compound provides a safe, natural environment where children can learn, play, and discover.

features.green: Large green grounds with play areas

classrooms.title: Classrooms
classrooms.description: Bright, modern classrooms designed for small-scale education with personal attention.

outdoor.title: Outdoor Spaces
outdoor.description: Extensive playgrounds, a vegetable garden, and green spaces for outdoor activities and nature education.

library.title: Library
library.description: A rich collection of Dutch and English books to stimulate reading enjoyment.

arts.title: Creative Spaces
arts.description: Spaces for art, music, cooking, and other creative activities.

directions.title: Directions
directions.content: We are easily accessible from all parts of Nairobi. Our school also offers transport from various neighborhoods.

cta.title: Visit Our Campus
cta.subtitle: Experience the peace and space of our green school grounds.

hero.title: Education
hero.subtitle: Bilingual education from toddler group through grade 8

intro.title: Our Educational Offering
intro.content: Dutch School Nairobi offers a complete educational pathway for children from age 1.5. Our bilingual curriculum combines the best of the Dutch educational system with international curricula.

toddler.description: A warm, playful environment where toddlers take their first steps in their learning journey. Maximum 15 children, 2-5 mornings per week.

primary.title: Primary School
primary.description: Fully bilingual education with Dutch curriculum and IPC. Small classes, personal attention.

ntc.title: NTC
ntc.description: For children at other international schools who want to learn or maintain their Dutch.

languageClub.description: French (groups 5-8) and Kiswahili (groups 1-8) as additional language offerings.

skillsClub.title: Skills Club
skillsClub.description: After-school activities: art, music, woodworking, gardening, and sports.

cta.subtitle: We're happy to help you find the best pathway for your child.

hero.title: Toddler Group
hero.subtitle: A warm start for our youngest learners, from age 1.5

intro.title: Learning Through Play
intro.content: Our toddler group provides a safe, stimulating environment where children from age 1.5 playfully become familiar with the Dutch language and culture. In a small group of maximum 15 children, every child receives the attention and care they deserve.

schedule.title: Flexible Care
schedule.description: Choose 2, 3, 4, or 5 mornings per week, depending on your family's needs.

language.title: Language Exposure
language.description: Natural introduction to Dutch through songs, stories, and daily interactions.

play.title: Learning Through Play
play.description: Development through play, creativity, and social interaction with peers.

observation.title: KIJK! Observation System
observation.description: We track each child's development with the KIJK! observation system.

activities.title: Daily Activities
list: Circle time with songs and stories

cta.title: Want to Learn More?

hero.title: Primary School
hero.subtitle: Bilingual education from groups 1 through 8

intro.title: The Best of Both Worlds
intro.content: Our primary school combines the Dutch curriculum with the International Primary Curriculum (IPC). Children receive education in both Dutch and English, preparing them optimally for either return to the Netherlands or continuation of international education.

kindergarten.title: Groups 1-2 (Kindergarten)
kindergarten.description: 3 days Dutch, 2 days English. Learning through play with the IEYC curriculum.

lower.title: Groups 3-5 (Lower Primary)
lower.description: Fundamental skills in language, mathematics, and world studies in both languages.

upper.title: Groups 6-8 (Upper Primary)
upper.description: Deepening knowledge and preparation for secondary education. Transition test in group 8.

assessment.title: Assessment & Tracking
assessment.content: We use the CITO student monitoring system to track each child's progress. In group 8, students take the official transition test.
items: CITO tests for comparison with Netherlands

cta.title: Want to Know More?
cta.subtitle: Discover how our bilingual education can prepare your child for the future.

hero.title: NTC - Dutch Language and Culture
hero.subtitle: For Dutch-speaking children at other international schools

intro.title: Maintain and Develop Dutch
intro.content: The NTC program is specially designed for children who attend another international school but want to maintain and develop their Dutch language and culture. Perfect for expat families who will later return to the Netherlands or Belgium.

forWhom.title: For Whom?
forWhom.content: The NTC program is intended for children of Dutch or Belgian families who:
items: Attend another international school in Nairobi
Want to maintain or improve their Dutch
Want to prepare for return to the Netherlands/Belgium
Want to stay connected to Dutch culture and traditions

language.title: Dutch Language
language.description: Lessons focused on reading, writing, speaking, and listening at level.

culture.title: Dutch Culture
culture.description: Attention to Dutch traditions, holidays, and current events.

levels.title: Level Groups
levels.description: Education tailored to the child's level and age.

schedule.title: Flexible Schedule
schedule.description: Lessons after school or on Wednesday afternoon.

cta.title: Interested in NTC?

hero.title: Student Support
hero.subtitle: Personal guidance for every child

intro.title: Every Child is Unique
intro.content: In our small-scale school, we know every child personally. Our student support is focused on supporting each child's development, with extra attention for those who need it.

ib.title: Learning Support Coordinator
ib.description: Our learning support coordinator coordinates student support, assists teachers, and guides children who need extra help.

observation.title: Observation & Signaling
observation.description: We systematically observe children to identify early when extra support is needed.

support.title: Appropriate Support
support.description: From extra instruction to classroom adaptations - we look for solutions that work for each child.

parents.title: Partnership with Parents
parents.description: Parents are partners in guiding their child. We share observations and align approaches.

assessment.title: Tracking Systems
assessment.content: We use recognized systems to track children's development and compare with Dutch standards.

kijk.title: KIJK!
kijk.description: Observation system for toddlers and kindergarteners that maps broad development.

cito.title: CITO LVS
cito.description: The Dutch student monitoring system for testing language and mathematics, comparable to schools in the Netherlands.

doorstroom.title: Transition Test
doorstroom.description: Official Dutch test in group 8 for advice toward secondary education.

pearson.title: Pearson English Benchmark

gifted.title: Giftedness

cta.title: Questions About Student Support?
cta.subtitle: We'd be happy to tell you more about how we guide children.

hero.subtitle: Lesson times and annual calendar

hero.title: School Transport
hero.subtitle: Safe transportation to and from school

intro.title: Our School Buses
intro.content: Dutch School Nairobi offers school bus transport from various parts of Nairobi. Our two routes cover the main areas where our families live.

route1.title: Route 1: Lavington / Kilimani
route1.description: This route serves the Lavington, Kilimani, and surrounding areas.

route2.title: Route 2: Loresho / Kitisuru
route2.description: This route serves the Loresho, Kitisuru, and surrounding areas.

safety.title: Safety
safety.content: Your child's safety is our priority. Our buses are equipped with seat belts and driven by experienced drivers. A supervisor is present during every trip.

practical.title: Practical Information
items: Pick-up and drop-off times are communicated at the beginning of the school year
Registration for bus transport goes through the school office
Bus transport is optional and separately priced

cta.title: Interested in School Transport?

hero.title: School Fees
hero.subtitle: Transparent fee structure

intro.title: Two Tariff Groups
intro.content: Dutch School Nairobi uses two tariff groups to keep education accessible for different family situations.

tariffA.title: Tariff 1
tariffA.description: For families where 75% or more of the school fees are paid by the employer.

tariffB.title: Tariff 2
tariffB.description: For families where less than 75% of the school fees are paid by the employer. Discounts apply for second and third children.

included.title: Included
items: Access to basic Skills Club activities

payment.title: Payment
payment.content: Tuition can be paid per term or per year. Payment arrangements can be discussed.

siblings.title: Discount
siblings.content: A discount applies for the second and subsequent children from the same family.

cta.title: Request Rates
cta.subtitle: Contact us for current rates and a personal conversation.

hero.title: Events
hero.subtitle: Dutch traditions and international celebrations

intro.title: A Year Full of Events
intro.content: At Dutch School Nairobi, we celebrate both Dutch traditions and international holidays. These events strengthen our community and connect children with their cultural background.

kerst.description: A joint Christmas celebration with performances by the children.

openDay.title: Open Day
openDay.description: Getting to know our school for interested families.

sportsDay.description: A day full of sports and games for all students.

endOfYear.description: Celebrating the end of the school year with performances and farewell to group 8.

calendar.content: The full events calendar is shared at the beginning of each school year. Parents receive timely invitations for all events.
